mnemonics: S.C.E.N.E: Stories Capture Every Needed Element. Use this to remember the components of a scenario: Stakeholders, Context, Exceptions, Needs, Epilogue/Ending.
keyPoints: Scenarios describe sequences of interactions between users and a system.
They are used to understand and model functional requirements from the user's perspective.
Effective scenarios include actors, a trigger, a flow of events, and a successful end condition.
commonMistakes: Focusing only on the 'happy path' and neglecting alternative or exception scenarios.
Writing scenarios that are too vague, lacking specific data, actions, or system responses.
Confusing scenarios with use cases; scenarios are specific instances, while use cases are general templates.
